title: The Scourge of Stonehill
setting: A secluded Rohan-style village, Stonehill, nestled in the foothills bordering the Misty Mountains, far from major settlements, known for its hearty folk and fine wool.
history: Stonehill has existed for centuries, a peaceful, self-sufficient community. Local folklore speaks of ancient dragons sleeping in the mountains, dismissed as old wives' tales. The village elder, Elara, possesses fragments of old lore hinting at a dormant evil.
atmosphere: Initially peaceful and rustic, quickly becoming tense and fearful with the looming threat of the dragon. Moments of quiet dread are punctuated by sudden, violent attacks. A sense of desperate hope and community resilience will permeate.
setup:
The Shadow Falls on Stonehill

The crisp mountain air of Stonehill normally carries the scent of pine and hearth smoke, a comforting aroma of a community at peace. For generations, the folk of Stonehill have tilled their fields, tended their sheep, and lived simple, honest lives in the shadow of the imposing Misty Mountains. The nearest town is days away, making Stonehill a secluded haven, untouched by the greater strife of Middle-earth.

However, tranquility has been shattered. A crimson terror has descended from the peaks – a young, but undeniably fearsome, dragon. Its first attack was sudden and brutal, scorching a portion of the village, sending panicked inhabitants scattering, and leaving a lingering smell of ash and fear. Livestock vanished, homes were reduced to embers, and the chilling roar of the beast now echoes frequently through the valleys.

The villagers, hardy as they are, are overwhelmed. Their spears and bows are little match for scales of iron and breath of fire. They look to anyone with courage and skill for aid, their ancient peace replaced by a primal fear. The party finds themselves in Stonehill, perhaps having just arrived, or residing there briefly, and are now eyewitnesses to this unfolding catastrophe.

startingPointImage.description: A small, rustic village in the foothills of mountains, with smoke rising from several buildings, and villagers looking up in fear at a distant, draconic shadow.
startingPointImage.prompt: A panoramic view of a small, medieval village nestled in a green valley at the base of craggy, snow-capped mountains. Several wooden houses are smoldering, with plumes of dark smoke rising. Villagers, dressed in simple tunics and cloaks, are looking up towards the mountain peaks with expressions of terror. A faint, dark silhouette of a dragon is visible against the clouds. Realistic, epic fantasy style, Middle-earth aesthetic, high detail.

mapImagePrompt.description: A hand-drawn style map of Stonehill village and its immediate surroundings, showing key locations and potential dragon attack paths.
mapImagePrompt.prompt: A detailed, hand-drawn fantasy map in the style of Middle-earth cartography. It depicts the village of Stonehill, showing individual houses, a central well, a market square, and a small temple. Surrounding the village are rolling hills, a forest edge, a winding river, and a path leading up into the jagged foothills of the Misty Mountains. Mark areas of recent dragon attacks with scorched symbols. Include a compass rose and a small legend in a classic fantasy font.

plotHooks.title: The Plea of the Elder
plotHooks.description:
The Plea of the Elder

Upon arriving at Stonehill, or waking to chaos, the party is approached by Elder Elara, a wise woman with lines of worry etched into her face. She recounts the terror, begs for assistance, and offers what little the village can spare for anyone brave enough to confront the beast or at least help evacuate survivors and recover supplies. She might offer an heirloom, a rare medicinal herb, or promise the gratitude of a grateful community.

plotHooks.title: Missing Kin
plotHooks.description:
Missing Kin

A frantic villager, perhaps a young farmer named Bran, approaches the party, weeping. His younger sister, Lyra, was tending sheep closer to the mountains when the dragon first struck, and she hasn't returned. He implores the party to help find her, fearing the worst but clinging to a sliver of hope that she might be hiding or injured. This hook immediately puts the party on the trail of the dragon's lair or recent activity.

plotHooks.title: Rumors of Treasure
plotHooks.description:
Rumors of Treasure

A grizzled trapper named Kael, who narrowly escaped the dragon's first attack, whispers tales of the beast carrying off a large, glimmering object towards a specific cave mouth in the mountains. He implies the dragon might be hoarding treasure, even if it's a young one, which could be a significant reward for those brave enough to retrieve it, or at least a way to lure the beast away.

mainPlotSteps.title: Session 1: The Ash and the Cry
mainPlotSteps.description:
Session 1: The Ash and the Cry

The party arrives in or witnesses the aftermath of Stonehill's first dragon attack. They interact with terrified villagers, particularly Elder Elara and those seeking missing loved ones. Their immediate task is to secure the village perimeter, aid the wounded, and investigate the initial attack site for clues on the dragon's size, type, and direction of flight. A smaller monster encounter (e.g., Goblins, Orc scouts) attracted by the chaos could occur, reinforcing the dangers of the wilds.

mainPlotSteps.title: Session 2: Trails of Terror
mainPlotSteps.description:
Session 2: Trails of Terror

Following clues from the first session (scorch marks, claw prints, frightened animals, or a villager's account), the party tracks the dragon's path deeper into the foothills. They discover its hunting grounds, perhaps finding a ravaged herd of mountain goats or evidence of its destructive foraging. They might stumble upon a mini-lair or a cache of minor stolen goods, leading to a skirmish with the dragon's territorial proxies (e.g., Mountain Orcs or Wargs who are also being displaced/affected by the dragon's presence).

mainPlotSteps.title: Session 3: The Dragon's Keep
mainPlotSteps.description:
Session 3: The Dragon's Keep

The party pinpoints the dragon's lair, likely a cave system high in the mountains. They must navigate treacherous terrain and potential traps or minor guardian creatures placed by the dragon. They discover the dragon's hoard – which, for a young dragon, might be smaller but still impressive, including some unique local treasures and perhaps the missing villager (Lyra) held captive. A confrontation with the dragon ensues, but it's not the final battle – perhaps it's a skirmish where the dragon assesses the threat, or it's injured and retreats.

mainPlotSteps.title: Session 4: The Scourge Vanquished
mainPlotSteps.description:
Session 4: The Scourge Vanquished

Having either wounded the dragon or driven it from its lair, the party must now prepare for a final confrontation. The dragon, enraged and perhaps weakened, might return to Stonehill for a vengeful final assault, forcing the villagers and party to enact a desperate defense. Alternatively, the party may have to lure it out or pursue it to a more advantageous battlefield. The module culminates in a climactic battle against the dragon, where the party's tactics and heroism determine the fate of Stonehill. The aftermath involves celebrating victory, rebuilding efforts, and receiving the village's gratitude.

mainCharacters.name: Elder Elara

image.description: An old, wise village elder with weathered skin and kind but resolute eyes, wearing simple, sturdy clothes.
image.prompt: A close-up portrait of an elderly woman, perhaps 70s, with deep wrinkles but alert, intelligent eyes. She wears a practical woolen tunic and a simple wooden brooch. Her hair is braided with silver streaks. Medieval fantasy, realistic, wise and stern expression, Middle-earth aesthetic, natural lighting.

mainCharacters.goal: Protect her people and rebuild Stonehill.
mainCharacters.quirk: Always carries a small, polished river stone for good luck.
mainCharacters.description:
Elara, Elder of Stonehill

Elara is the matriarch of Stonehill, her face weathered by years of sun and wind, but her eyes sharp and full of unwavering resolve. She has led the village through harsh winters and lean harvests, but nothing prepared her for a dragon. She is a fount of local knowledge and folklore, believing in ancient prophecies and signs. She feels a deep personal responsibility for every villager and will sacrifice much to ensure their safety. She carries a small, smooth river stone, a keepsake from her childhood, which she believes brings luck in times of trouble.

mainCharacters.name: Bran Stonehand

image.description: A young, distraught farmer, visibly worried and desperate, clutching a broken piece of wood.
image.prompt: A young man, early 20s, with a rugged, honest face streaked with dirt and worry. He has short, practical brown hair and is dressed in simple, patched farmer's clothes. His hands are calloused, and he nervously clutches a splintered wooden staff. Expression of anguish and determination. Medieval fantasy, realistic, Middle-earth peasant aesthetic.

mainCharacters.goal: Find his sister Lyra, alive or dead.
mainCharacters.quirk: Fidgets with a broken piece of a shepherd's crook.
mainCharacters.description:
Bran Stonehand, Young Farmer

Bran is a sturdy, earnest young farmer, barely old enough to shave, but whose hands are calloused from years of working the land. The dragon attack has left him distraught, especially since his younger sister, Lyra, is missing. He is desperate and willing to do anything to find her, offering what little coin or family heirlooms he has to the party. He's not cowardly, but lacks the skills to face the dragon himself. When stressed, he constantly fiddles with a splintered piece of his old shepherd's crook, a reminder of his lost flock and sister.

mainCharacters.name: Kael the Trapper

image.description: A grizzled, wary trapper with keen eyes and a lean build, dressed for the wilderness.
image.prompt: A lean, wiry man in his 40s or 50s, with a weathered face, a short, practical beard, and intense, observant eyes. He wears worn leather armor and carries a small pouch at his belt. His posture is slightly hunched, suggesting a life in the wilderness. He has a shrewd, cautious expression. Medieval fantasy, realistic, ranger/trapper aesthetic, Middle-earth.

mainCharacters.goal: Survive and perhaps profit from the chaos.
mainCharacters.quirk: Always has a pouch of dried berries and nuts, and offers them to anyone he talks to.
mainCharacters.description:
Kael the Trapper

Kael is a lean, wiry man with keen eyes that have seen much of the wild. He lives on the fringes of Stonehill, making his living from trapping and hunting in the foothills. He's gruff and wary of outsiders, but the dragon has forced him closer to the village for safety. He witnessed the dragon's flight path and might know old paths or forgotten caves in the mountains, making him a valuable, if reluctant, guide. He habitually chews on dried berries, offering them as a strange form of conversational punctuation.

monsters.name: Ignis, the Young Wyrm

image.description: A young, but fearsome red dragon with vibrant crimson scales and glowing eyes, perched on a rocky outcrop.
image.prompt: A vibrant red dragon, clearly young but still imposing, with smooth, crimson scales and sharp, intelligent eyes that glow with an inner fire. It has leathery wings and a long, muscular tail. It is perched on a jagged, smoking rock formation, surveying a mountain pass. High fantasy, realistic, fearsome but majestic. Middle-earth style dragon.

monsters.Traits: Darkvision 120 ft., Passive Perception 14. Legendary Resistance (1/Day): If Ignis fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead. Fiery Breath (Recharge 5-6): Ignis exhales fire in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, taking 4d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. This CR and HP is adjusted for a party of 4 level 2 characters, assuming tactical engagement or prior weakening of the beast.
monsters.description: Ignis is a young but ambitious red dragon, driven by instinct and a desire to establish its own territory. It's not fully mature, but still possesses formidable fire breath and thick scales. It revels in destruction and the fear it inspires, slowly building a hoard. While intelligent, its youth makes it prone to overconfidence and rage. Its scales are a vibrant crimson, and its eyes glow with an inner fire. It's an imposing, terrifying sight for the villagers.

monsters.name: Mountain Orc Scout

image.description: A grimy, brutish mountain orc with crude armor and a sneering expression.
image.prompt: A grotesque, green-skinned orc, lean and muscular, with tusks protruding from its lower jaw. It wears crude, patched leather armor and carries a rusty scimitar. Its eyes are beady and malicious, and its expression is a snarl. It stands in a rocky, barren landscape. Middle-earth style orc, gritty realism.

monsters.Traits: Darkvision 60 ft. Aggressive: As a bonus action, the orc can move up to its speed toward a hostile creature that it can see.
monsters.description: These crude, brutish orcs have been driven from their usual hunting grounds by the dragon's presence and now scavenge near Stonehill, taking advantage of the chaos. They are opportunistic and dangerous, armed with rusty scimitars and javelins. They're more nuisance than major threat, but can overwhelm an unwary party if encountered in numbers.

rewardsImagePrompt.description: A small pile of gold, a few silver coins, some rustic village trinkets, and a gleaming, unique magic item, possibly a worn short sword or a leather bracer.
rewardsImagePrompt.prompt: A close-up of a small, rustic wooden table piled with rewards. On it are a few stacks of tarnished gold coins, a handful of silver pieces, a small pouch of dried herbs, a simple carved wooden bird, and a prominent, slightly glowing short sword with an elven design on its hilt. The background is blurred, focusing on the treasures. High fantasy, realistic, Middle-earth aesthetic.

rewardsList.name: Gratitude of Stonehill
rewardsList.description:
Gratitude of Stonehill

The eternal thanks of Elder Elara and the villagers of Stonehill, offering safe haven, food, and lodging whenever the party returns. They will also provide free repairs for armor and weapons, and share any rare herbs or local knowledge they possess.

rewardsList.name: Dragon's Hoard
rewardsList.description:
Dragon's Hoard

A modest hoard for a young dragon, including 300 gp, 500 sp, and various pieces of stolen household silver (worth another 100 gp). It also contains a finely woven tapestry depicting a battle between ancient men and orcs (worth 150 gp) and a small, strangely warm stone that glows faintly (a minor magic item, perhaps a 'Warmth Stone' providing resistance to cold for an hour once per day).

rewardsList.name: Village Support
rewardsList.description:
Village Support

Upon defeating the dragon, the villagers will band together to provide the party with provisions, healing supplies, and perhaps a small, reliable riding horse each as a token of their immense gratitude, should the party need to travel.

generatedMagicItems.name: Stonehill Defender's Blade

image.description: A simple iron shortsword with a worn leather grip, faintly glowing with an inner light.
image.prompt: A simple, utilitarian shortsword with a sturdy iron blade and a plain leather-wrapped hilt. A subtle, faint magical glow emanates from the blade. It looks well-used but cared for. Fantasy weapon art, realistic, Middle-earth style.

generatedMagicItems.description:
Stonehill Defender's Blade

Weapon (shortsword), uncommon

This simple iron shortsword was once wielded by Stonehill's first protector, imbued with a minor defensive enchantment. When you hit a creature with this magic weapon, you gain 1 temporary hit point. This effect can occur once per turn. The blade feels strangely light and hums softly when danger is near.

generatedMagicItems.name: Elara's Charm of Steadfastness

image.description: A small, intricately carved wooden amulet depicting a mountain ram, strung on a leather cord.
image.prompt: A small, round wooden amulet, intricately carved with the image of a majestic mountain ram. It is strung on a simple, braided leather cord. The wood is dark and smooth from wear. Fantasy artifact, realistic, folk art style, Middle-earth aesthetic.

generatedMagicItems.description:
Elara's Charm of Steadfastness

Wondrous item, uncommon (requires attunement)

This small, intricately carved wooden amulet, gifted by Elder Elara, depicts a mountain ram. While wearing this charm, you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened. Additionally, once per long rest, you can use an action to gain a +1 bonus to your AC for 1 minute.

generatedMagicItems.name: Wyrmscale Gauntlets

image.description: A pair of light gauntlets crafted from reddish-brown dragon scales, supple yet strong.
image.prompt: A pair of light, fingerless gauntlets made from small, overlapping reddish-brown dragon scales stitched onto sturdy leather. They appear flexible and durable, with a faint sheen. A subtle wisp of smoke seems to linger near them. Fantasy armor, realistic, Middle-earth style.

generatedMagicItems.description:
Wyrmscale Gauntlets

Armor (light gauntlets), uncommon

Fashioned from a few shed scales of Ignis, these gauntlets provide surprisingly durable protection. While wearing these gauntlets, you gain a +1 bonus to your AC. They are warm to the touch and possess a faint, lingering scent of smoke.

generatedPlotTwists.title: The Dragon's True Master
generatedPlotTwists.description:
The Dragon's True Master

The young dragon Ignis is not acting alone. It is being subtly influenced or even commanded by a more sinister, unseen force (e.g., a disillusioned hermit, a goblin shaman, or even an ancient, malevolent spirit stirred by the dragon's presence) who seeks to cause chaos in the region for their own nefarious ends. This twist could be revealed by finding a strange amulet in the dragon's lair or by observing peculiar behavior from the beast.

generatedPlotTwists.title: The Elder's Secret Lore
generatedPlotTwists.description:
The Elder's Secret Lore

Elder Elara has been holding back crucial information. She knows of an ancient ritual or forgotten artifact (perhaps alluded to in the fragments of lore she possesses) that could banish or weaken a dragon, but it comes with a terrible cost or requires a dangerous quest to retrieve its components. She might reveal this secret only when the situation seems dire, testing the party's resolve.

generatedPlotTwists.title: The Dragon is Protecting Something
generatedPlotTwists.description:
The Dragon is Protecting Something

The dragon's destructive rampages are not purely malicious. It is, in fact, trying to protect something valuable or sentient within its lair or a nearby hidden location from an even greater, more immediate threat. Perhaps it guards a forgotten dwarven treasure vault from invading orcs, or a rare magical sapling from those who would exploit it. This adds moral complexity to simply slaying the beast.
